svg.title: Wave superposition: constructive, destructive, and partial interference for three phase differences
svg.desc: Three stacked panels showing the superposition of two equal-amplitude waves (ochre and gray dashed) with phase differences delta=0, pi, and pi/2. The resultant sum (solid red) has amplitude 2A for delta=0 (fully constructive), zero for delta=pi (fully destructive, flat line), and A*sqrt(2) for delta=pi/2 (partial). Amplitude indicators mark the peak in each panel.

cajal:figure.figure-title: Superposition: constructive, destructive, and partial interference
cajal:figure.author: Humanitarians AI Incorporated

svg.text: Superposition A cos(kx) + A cos(kx + delta) — amplitude 2A |cos(delta/2)|
